reviews.review_description: Very basic graphics. I like the magic stick effect, but the gameplay and interface are not very good. I would skip this one until they finish the project. Looks like they're midway.

I had to download this game because of the name. It has a jovial, adventurous feel to it, and I was looking forward to chasing down the wizard through low-poly countryside...
However, all of the story was limited to a single awkwardly paced "cutscene", and then we leave the rural village for a series of floating platforms with a castle aesthetic floating impossibly high within a country-side skybox.

I was so disappointed. The title had reminded me of the many point-and-click "Quest" adventure games, with their tongue-in-cheek attitude to fantasy storytelling. But instead of traipsing around the county, we get an arcade-y ball-bouncing minigame with a medieval theme. And whilst the first-person perspective is immersing (insomuch as VR always immerses you in your virtual environs), there's nothing and no one to see in this world; and it makes the game that much harder, since you can't see the whole field that you're bouncing your magical teleport-ball around.

At the end of the day, the best thing I can say about this game is that it has a good title. Unfortunately, that just makes it all the more disappointing that they squandered the opportunity for a jovial fantasy adventure for some kind of minigolf game with low graphics. Very disappointing.
